title: Phonics & Word Reading
objective: Students will explore phonics & word reading concepts including Alternative Spellings for Known Sounds, Reading with Expression and Accuracy, Syllables (age 6+).

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Alternative Spellings for Known Sounds!

Recognise alternative grapheme-phoneme correspondences for known phonemes (e.g., /dʒ/ as ge/dge/g, /s/ as c, /n/ as kn/gn, /r/ as wr, /ʒ/ as s), reading words with less common spellings for familiar sounds
Let's practice together! Read words with less common spellings for familiar sounds: 'badge', 'giraffe', 'city', 'knock', 'gnaw', 'write', 'treasure'
Does you know that the /j/ sound can be spelled different ways — like in 'jump', 'cage', and 'bridge' — and can they read words with these less common spellings?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Alternative Spellings for Known Sounds: Read words with less common spellings for familiar sounds: 'badge', 'giraffe', 'city', 'knock', 'gnaw', 'write', 'treasure' Identify that the same phoneme can be represented by different graphemes (e.g., /dʒ/ spelled g, ge, dge)
Today we're going to learn about Reading with Expression and Accuracy. Get ready for an adventure!
Read aloud with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression (prosody), re-reading familiar texts to build fluency and confidence, and using context to self-correct
Time to try this out: Read a grade-level passage aloud with expression and appropriate pacing
When you reads a story aloud, do they vary their voice to match the mood — pausing at punctuation, slowing for sad parts, speeding up for excitement?

Great job! Today we learned about Reading with Expression and Accuracy. Read a grade-level passage aloud with expression and appropriate pacing Self-correct miscues during oral reading by re-reading or using context cues
Have you ever thought about Syllables (age 6+)? Let's find out together!
Decode words of two or more syllables by breaking them into syllable chunks, applying knowledge that every syllable must contain a vowel sound, and blending the parts together
Let's practice together! Read 'rabbit', 'thunder', 'fantastic' by breaking into syllable chunks and blending
When you encounters a long word like 'fantastic' or 'umbrella', can they break it into syllables and blend the parts together to read the whole word?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Syllables (age 6+): Read 'rabbit', 'thunder', 'fantastic' by breaking into syllable chunks and blending Clap or tap syllables in a multi-syllable word, then read each chunk before blending
